lesson.menu_desc: Reading a product roadmap + future forms -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Product Calls & Roadmap
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita | Aula 12 -- Product Calls & Roadmap

reading.rtitle: Service Cloud — Q3 Product Roadmap & Release Notes
paras: This roadmap shows what our product team is going to build over the next three months, and when each part will reach you. Our goal is simple: to deliver the features you have asked for and to make the product easier to use every day. Nothing here is a promise of an exact date, but the plan is clear, and we are going to keep you updated at every step.

Let me walk you through the highlights. In June, we are going to release version three of the dashboard. This release includes two enhancements that many customers have requested: faster reports and a cleaner mobile view. We are also launching a brand-new mobile app on Tuesday the fifteenth, so your agents will be able to work from anywhere. Each feature has a clear scope, and our team is testing them right now.

Of course, we cannot build everything at once. We prioritize the requests with the biggest impact first, and the rest stay on our backlog for a future release. If a feature is not on this roadmap yet, it does not mean we will forget it; it means we are still working on the estimate. Next quarter we are going to review the backlog with you and decide together what comes next. Thank you for helping us shape the product.
reading.source: Adapted for class use from a SaaS product roadmap and release notes

gist.prompt: What is the best one-line summary of this roadmap?
choices: The product is broken, so the team is going to stop building any new features.
A clear plan of the features and releases the team is going to deliver, and how it prioritizes customer requests.
A final price list for the new mobile app and the dashboard upgrade.

items: The team is going to release version three of the dashboard in June.
The text says: "In June, we are going to release version three of the dashboard."
The new mobile app has already launched and is available to everyone now.
The app has not launched yet; they are launching it on Tuesday the fifteenth, in the future.
Requests with the biggest impact are prioritized before the others.
The team prioritizes the requests with the biggest impact first; the rest stay on the backlog.
If a feature is not on the roadmap, the team will never build it.
Not being on the roadmap does not mean they will forget it; they are still working on the estimate.
In your team, how do you decide which customer requests to prioritize first?
The roadmap avoids promising an exact date. Why is that careful wording important with customers?
A customer asks for a feature that is only on the backlog. How would you answer them honestly?

listenings.text: Hi Patricia, this is Marcus from the product team, leaving you a quick voice note before your call with the customer, so we are aligned on the roadmap. Here is the short version you can share. In June, we are going to release version three of the dashboard, and that release includes the two enhancements your client has asked for the most: faster reports and a cleaner mobile view. On top of that, we are launching the new mobile app on Tuesday the fifteenth, so their agents will finally be able to work from their phones. Now, please be careful with dates on the call. Say that this is the plan, not a fixed promise, because the final rollout still depends on testing. If they push for the contact feature they requested last month, be honest: it is on the backlog, we have not built it yet, and we are still working on the estimate. What you can promise is that we are going to review the backlog with them next quarter and prioritize together. So lead with the wins, set the right expectation on the dates, and tell them we will keep them updated at every step. Good luck, and let me know how it goes.

Hi Patricia, this is Sofia, the admin on the customer side, leaving you a short message after I read your release notes. First, thank you, the roadmap is really clear, and my team is excited about the new dashboard. The faster reports are exactly the enhancement we requested, so that is great news. I do have a couple of questions for our call. The mobile app you mentioned, are you launching it for all users on the fifteenth, or only for a small group first? My agents are asking, because some of them want to start using it straight away. My second question is about a feature we asked for back in March, a way to merge duplicate contacts. I cannot find it on this roadmap. Is it still on your backlog, or have you decided not to build it? I am not upset either way, I just need an honest answer and a rough estimate, so I can manage my own team's expectations. If it is going to take a few more months, that is fine, I just want to know. Could you bring those two points to the call on Thursday? Thanks so much, and again, the team is really happy with this release.
